claim: The cgl-publish script correctly uploads a built surface directory to the Cloudflare R2 CDN bucket and writes a provenance.json manifest alongside the artifact, verified via dry-run against the staging bucket.
support: Ran 'bin/cgl-publish --dry-run surfaces/snake/ --dest r2://studio-cdn-staging/snake/' and inspected output: upload manifest listed 8 files, provenance.json was written with bundle_id, lab_slug, published_at, and sha256 checksums. Exit code 0.

claim: The publish pipeline preserves the claw bundle_id as the CDN path prefix, enabling direct tracing from a live URL back to the originating artifact bundle.
support: CDN path schema is r2://studio-cdn/<lab_slug>/<bundle_id>/<surface_files>. Verified in dry-run output that the snake surface uploaded to r2://studio-cdn-staging/surface-snake/20260427-091500-builder-surface-snake/index.html.
